mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals weakening labor demand and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in over 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and housing starts indicates broad-based economic strength and rising consumer leverage, which sustains equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects capacity constraints and rising input costs, which erode profit margins and trigger short-term S&P 500 sell-offs.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth rates yield positive 5-day forward returns in late-cycle regimes, contradicting the margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, high discount rates and tighter financial conditions suppress equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 70% of observations when FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline shows a negative or insignificant coefficient in predicting 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in retail sales during post-shock recovery phases signal fragile consumer confidence and immediate earnings downgrades, causing rapid negative S&P 500 price adjustments within one week.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS growth fails to predict negative 5-day returns in post-shock regimes, with a hit rate below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher consumer price index readings increase inflation expectations and force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases correlate with positive 20-day forward returns in over 60% of monthly observations, invalidating the inflation-risk channel.
